what_it_does: A CSS stylesheet that defines the visual styling and layout for the ConceptV design system's universal type registry, type builder, architecture canvas, workshop studio floor, inspector, modal dialogs, and per-layer thumbnail previews.
observations: Large monolithic stylesheet with no imports or resets
Heavy use of CSS custom properties for a warm ivory/gold color theme
Class namespaces include cv- (registry/components), tb- (type builder), and rs- (registry studio floor)
Defines many UI patterns: badges, pills, chips, cards, trees, filters, grids, sticky rails, modals, and chat bubbles
Includes a detailed TypeThumb taxonomy with separate preview classes for token/atom/block/system/surface/doc/template layers
Uses CSS Grid and Flexbox extensively, plus sticky positioning for side panels
Contains keyframe animations for inspector slide-in and modal appearance
Uses :has() for conditional workshop tile layout
Responsive breakpoints reduce tile counts and hide example thumbnails on smaller viewports
Some classes are defined or overridden in multiple places within the same file
standouts: Universal TypeThumb system with dedicated preview classes for each layer/family
Workshop Studio Floor (rs-) with shelf tiles, sliding inspector, and Vi chat rail
Type Builder three-pane split layout with preview center and inspector right rail
Architecture canvas ladder with rank bars, counts, and embedded example thumbnails
Provenance-driven styling via modifier classes (built-in, user, vi, imported)
conventions: CSS custom properties for colors, radii, shadows, fonts, and easing
Consistent 120–140 ms transition durations on interactive elements
Uppercase micro-labels achieved with letter-spacing and text-transform
Sticky side panels with max-height and overflow-y: auto
Grid layouts using minmax(0, 1fr) to prevent overflow
Class modifiers for provenance, density, and active/selected states
concerns: File is very long and monolithic, mixing many subsystems in one stylesheet
Some classes are redefined later with conflicting property values, e.g. .cv-type-card and .cv-reg-counts
:has() selector has limited browser support and may break older browsers
Hardcoded literal colors appear in some thumb previews (#555, #fff, #ddd, #988058, #5B4628) alongside CSS variables
No comments explaining the semantic meaning of the seven layers or custom property values
Magic numbers and fixed widths/heights throughout could hinder maintainability
notes: This is the presentation-layer CSS for a design-system registry and builder tool, organized by functional area and themed with warm gold/ivory tokens.
questions: What are the exact semantics of the seven registry layers referenced by .cv-reg-counts?
Where are the CSS custom properties such as --accent-gold and --font-display defined?
Are the TypeThumb preview classes rendered statically or generated dynamically by JavaScript?
What is the intended relationship between the .tb- (TypeBuilder) and .rs- (Registry Studio) namespaces?
How is the Vi assistant integrated with the type builder and inspector?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it styles a universal type registry, type builder, architecture canvas, and workshop studio floor.
apparent_themes: Warm editorial aesthetic using ivory, gold, and bronze tones
Provenance and layer visibility as first-class UI concerns
Studio/workshop metaphor for composing design-system artifacts
Human-AI collaboration through a Vi assistant rail and chat UI
intention_signals: Build a visually rich, thumb-first registry where design-system types can be browsed, inspected, composed, and created either manually or with AI assistance, with clear provenance and layer taxonomy.
novelty: A comprehensive CSS taxonomy for design-system 'type' artifacts that pairs every layer/family with a dedicated thumbnail preview and supports a studio-floor shelf/inspector interaction model.
contribution: A self-contained visual vocabulary and layout system for a type registry UI, including thumbnail previews, builder panes, architecture diagrams, and an inspector panel, all themed consistently via CSS variables.
summary_for_embedding: A CSS stylesheet implementing the visual design of a universal type registry, type builder, architecture canvas, and workshop studio floor with warm ivory/gold tokens, thumbnail previews, and inspector layouts.
intention_for_embedding: To provide the styling needed for browsing, inspecting, and constructing design-system types and templates in a unified studio interface.
